title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony and Mr. Issacson discuss the suspicious confinement of the guests following the death of the prince, which was officially attributed to heart failure. Anthony notes that the weapon used in the crime has not been found and suspects that Constable Batters' claimed trip to London might be a ruse. Later, Virginia and Lord Caterham join the group, and Virginia invites Anthony to a secret meeting at the boathouse.

During the meeting at the center of the lake, Virginia, Bill, and Anthony discuss the previous night's events. Anthony suspects the intruders were searching for a small item rather than a memoir, noting that their interest in a wall panel suggested they were looking for a secret staircase. They discuss several suspicious individuals, including Esakistan, who has been sleeping excessively, and Perry, Michael's servant. Anthony predicts that the intruders will return that night to take advantage of Batters' absence.

The trio forms a task force to lie in wait. Anthony, carrying a handgun, watches the gate while Virginia hides behind a suit of armor and Bill stays by the window. In the early hours of the morning, a man crawls through a window and uses a flashlight to scan the room. As the man approaches the panel he had previously inspected, Bill is unable to suppress a sneeze. Anthony lunges at the intruder, and Virginia turns on the lights. The man is revealed to be a well-dressed, bearded stranger previously seen at the Happy Ballplayer Inn. Sheriff Batters suddenly appears in the doorway, revealing he only pretended to go to London. The stranger identifies himself as being from Scotland Yard and presents his credentials to Batters.
